sanskrit: श्रीभगवानुवाच | अनाश्रितः कर्मफलं कार्यं कर्म करोति यः | स संन्यासी च योगी च न निरग्निर्न चाक्रियः ||
transliteration: śrī-bhagavān uvāca | anāśritaḥ karma-phalaṃ kāryaṃ karma karoti yaḥ | sa sannyāsī ca yogī ca na niragnir na cākriyaḥ ||
literal_meaning: The Lord said: One who performs obligatory work without taking shelter of the fruit of action—he is a true renunciant and a true yogi; not the one who is without fire (ritual) and without activity.
contextual_meaning: Authentic renunciation is an inner stance of non-attachment to outcomes, not outer abstinence. Psychological health is measured by intrinsic duty-orientation, not by withdrawal from action.
psychological_state: Arjuna feels the tension between societal images of holiness (ascetic withdrawal) and his warrior duty; he is being guided toward an integrated self-concept that allows engaged action without egoic craving.
trigger_scenario: A high-achieving professional torn between quitting to 'find peace' and staying to serve without obsessing over promotions.
anchor_text: Do your duty without clinging to the outcome.
